ai-readiness-api-description:0.message: API description must be present and at least 50 characters for AI readiness
ai-readiness-api-description:0.description: API must have a comprehensive description for AI understanding
ai-readiness-api-description:0.fixSuggestion: Expand `info.description` to at least 50 characters: scope, main resources, auth model, and links to external docs or portals.

ai-readiness-path-item-summary:1.message: Path item should include a summary for better AI route understanding
ai-readiness-path-item-summary:1.description: Path items should have summaries to provide route-level context
ai-readiness-path-item-summary:1.fixSuggestion: Set `summary` on the path item to describe the resource or route group before drilling into per-method details.

ai-readiness-list-pagination-params:2.message: GET operation should declare pagination parameters (cursor/page and limit) for AI agent iteration over large result sets
ai-readiness-list-pagination-params:2.description: GET operations should support standard pagination parameters
ai-readiness-list-pagination-params:2.fixSuggestion: Add query parameters such as `limit` plus `cursor` or `page` (and document defaults and max) on list-style GETs.

ai-readiness-operation-description:3.message: Operation 'get' description must be present and at least 30 characters for AI readiness
ai-readiness-operation-description:3.description: All operations must have a detailed description
ai-readiness-operation-description:3.fixSuggestion: Write at least 30 characters in `description`: inputs, outcomes, idempotency, and notable errors or side effects.

ai-readiness-operation-id:4.message: Operation must have an operationId for AI readiness
ai-readiness-operation-id:4.description: All operations must have an operationId for AI reference
ai-readiness-operation-id:4.fixSuggestion: Set a unique `operationId` per operation for stable cross-references in code generators and agent prompts.

ai-readiness-operation-tags:5.message: Operation should have at least one tag for AI categorization
ai-readiness-operation-tags:5.description: All operations should have tags for AI categorization
ai-readiness-operation-tags:5.fixSuggestion: Add `tags` that reference your global `tags` list so operations group correctly in docs and AI context windows.

ai-readiness-error-responses-4xx:6.message: Operation must define at least one 4xx error response for AI error handling
ai-readiness-error-responses-4xx:6.fixSuggestion: Document the client and auth failure cases you support (e.g. 400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 429) with bodies agents can parse.

ai-readiness-error-responses-5xx:7.message: Operation should define at least one 5xx error response for AI error handling
ai-readiness-error-responses-5xx:7.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` (or `502`/`503`/`504` as appropriate) response with a schema for generic or upstream failures.

ai-readiness-response-example:8.message: Response should have an example for AI understanding
ai-readiness-response-example:8.fixSuggestion: Under each 2xx response media type, add `example` or `examples` for a representative success payload.

ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:9.message: GET list operation response schema should include pagination metadata (has_more, next_cursor, or total) so AI agents know when to stop paginating
ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:9.description: List endpoint responses must include pagination metadata
ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:9.fixSuggestion: Expose at least one of `has_more`, `next_cursor`/`next_page`, or `total` in the JSON body schema for paginated lists.
ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:9.displayPath: paths > /hello > get > responses > 200 > content > application/json > schema

ai-readiness-path-item-summary:10.message: Path item should include a summary for better AI route understanding
ai-readiness-path-item-summary:10.description: Path items should have summaries to provide route-level context
ai-readiness-path-item-summary:10.fixSuggestion: Set `summary` on the path item to describe the resource or route group before drilling into per-method details.

ai-readiness-list-pagination-params:11.message: GET operation should declare pagination parameters (cursor/page and limit) for AI agent iteration over large result sets
ai-readiness-list-pagination-params:11.description: GET operations should support standard pagination parameters
ai-readiness-list-pagination-params:11.fixSuggestion: Add query parameters such as `limit` plus `cursor` or `page` (and document defaults and max) on list-style GETs.

ai-readiness-operation-description:12.message: Operation 'get' description must be present and at least 30 characters for AI readiness
ai-readiness-operation-description:12.description: All operations must have a detailed description
ai-readiness-operation-description:12.fixSuggestion: Write at least 30 characters in `description`: inputs, outcomes, idempotency, and notable errors or side effects.

ai-readiness-operation-id:13.message: Operation must have an operationId for AI readiness
ai-readiness-operation-id:13.description: All operations must have an operationId for AI reference
ai-readiness-operation-id:13.fixSuggestion: Set a unique `operationId` per operation for stable cross-references in code generators and agent prompts.

ai-readiness-operation-tags:14.message: Operation should have at least one tag for AI categorization
ai-readiness-operation-tags:14.description: All operations should have tags for AI categorization
ai-readiness-operation-tags:14.fixSuggestion: Add `tags` that reference your global `tags` list so operations group correctly in docs and AI context windows.

ai-readiness-parameter-example:15.message: Parameter '' should have an example for AI understanding
ai-readiness-parameter-example:15.description: Parameters should have examples for AI understanding
ai-readiness-parameter-example:15.fixSuggestion: Add `example` or `examples` on query, path, header, and cookie parameters so agents can send realistic values.

ai-readiness-parameter-description:16.message: Parameter '' description must be present and at least 15 characters for AI readiness
ai-readiness-parameter-description:16.description: All parameters must have meaningful descriptions
ai-readiness-parameter-description:16.fixSuggestion: Document each parameter: meaning, format, units, valid ranges, defaults, and when it is required — at least 15 characters.

ai-readiness-error-responses-5xx:17.message: Operation should define at least one 5xx error response for AI error handling
ai-readiness-error-responses-5xx:17.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` (or `502`/`503`/`504` as appropriate) response with a schema for generic or upstream failures.

ai-readiness-response-description:18.message: Response '200' description must be present and at least 20 characters for AI readiness
ai-readiness-response-description:18.description: All responses must have meaningful descriptions
ai-readiness-response-description:18.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` (≥20 chars) to each response code explaining when it is returned, what the body represents, and what the client should do.

ai-readiness-response-example:19.message: Response should have an example for AI understanding
ai-readiness-response-example:19.fixSuggestion: Under each 2xx response media type, add `example` or `examples` for a representative success payload.

ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:20.message: GET list operation response schema should include pagination metadata (has_more, next_cursor, or total) so AI agents know when to stop paginating
ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:20.description: List endpoint responses must include pagination metadata
ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:20.fixSuggestion: Expose at least one of `has_more`, `next_cursor`/`next_page`, or `total` in the JSON body schema for paginated lists.
ai-readiness-pagination-response-meta:20.displayPath: paths > /users/{id} > get > responses > 200 > content > application/json > schema

ai-readiness-error-response-content:21.message: Error response '[404].content' must define response content so AI agents can parse failures consistently
ai-readiness-error-response-content:21.description: All error responses must define structured payload content
ai-readiness-error-response-content:21.fixSuggestion: For 4xx/5xx responses, add `content` with at least one media type so errors are not undocumented plain text.

ai-readiness-response-description:22.message: Response '404' description must be present and at least 20 characters for AI readiness
ai-readiness-response-description:22.description: All responses must have meaningful descriptions
ai-readiness-response-description:22.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` (≥20 chars) to each response code explaining when it is returned, what the body represents, and what the client should do.

categories.label: Semantic Discovery
categories.description: Can AI agents find the right endpoint and understand its intent?
categories.whyItMatters: AI agents rely on summaries, descriptions, and stable operation identifiers to select the right tool for a task. Without them, agents must infer behavior through trial and error, leading to incorrect calls and hallucinated responses.

subBuckets.label: Summaries
subBuckets.description: Clear operation summaries help agents pick the right endpoint quickly.

subBuckets.label: Descriptions
subBuckets.description: Detailed descriptions reduce ambiguity in agent execution flows.

subBuckets.label: Operation IDs
subBuckets.description: Stable operation IDs improve deterministic tool calling for agents.

categories.label: Contract Integrity
categories.description: Can AI agents construct valid requests and interpret responses without guessing?
categories.whyItMatters: Agents generate payloads based on schemas and examples. Ambiguous types, missing required fields, or absent examples cause invalid requests or misinterpretation of responses.

subBuckets.label: Examples
subBuckets.description: Request and response examples help agents construct valid payloads.

subBuckets.label: Strict Typing
subBuckets.description: Strong typing keeps agent-generated requests aligned with schema constraints.

subBuckets.label: Responses
subBuckets.description: Defined success and error responses help agents interpret outcomes.

categories.label: Resilience & Recovery
categories.description: Can AI agents handle failures, rate limits, and large datasets gracefully?
categories.whyItMatters: Autonomous agents operate without human supervision. Error schemas enable self-correction, rate limit headers prevent hammering, and pagination metadata indicates when to stop iterating.

subBuckets.label: Error Semantics
subBuckets.description: Structured, actionable error details for AI self-correction.

subBuckets.label: Rate Limit Headers
subBuckets.description: Rate limit and retry headers prevent unsafe autonomous request bursts.

subBuckets.label: Pagination
subBuckets.description: Pagination metadata helps agents iterate large datasets safely.

categories.label: Security & Integrity
categories.description: Is the API safe for autonomous agent access over the long term?
categories.whyItMatters: Agents cannot complete interactive OAuth flows. Undefined security risks unintended state changes. Idempotency prevents duplicate side-effects on retries.

subBuckets.label: Agent Auth
subBuckets.description: Explicit security requirements reduce risk in autonomous access.

subBuckets.label: Idempotency
subBuckets.description: Idempotency protection avoids duplicate side effects on retries.

dimensions.label: Security & Integrity
dimensions.description: Is the API safe for autonomous agent access over the long term?
dimensions.whyItMatters: Agents cannot complete interactive browser-based OAuth flows. Undefined security requirements risk agents making unintended state changes. Idempotency support prevents duplicate side-effects when agents retry operations.

metadata.name: REST API AI Readiness Guidelines
metadata.description: This ruleset ensures that REST APIs are well-documented and structured to be easily understood and utilized by AI systems. It checks for comprehensive descriptions, examples, and proper structuring of API components to enhance AI readiness.
